root_cause: The kneeboard flickering issue was likely due to the swapchain not being updated consistently during rendering, which can cause visual artifacts in VR applications.
fix: The patch introduced a new quirk option, `OpenXR_AlwaysUpdateSwapchain`, allowing the swapchain to be updated on every game frame, thereby reducing flickering.
causal_chain: Inconsistent swapchain updates → Kneeboard flickers in DCS MT → Introduced `OpenXR_AlwaysUpdateSwapchain` to ensure swapchain updates on every frame.

text: Fixed audio devices swapping on device hw changes | When audio devices connected or disconnected from the system, the default audio device swapped around. This was because of QML setting index 0 on Co

fix: The patch added a call to `virtualkeyboard_release_keys()` at the beginning of the `virtualkeyboard_step()` function to ensure keys are released regardless of the keyboard's visibility.
causal_chain: Keys not released in the previous frame → Soft keyboard buttons remain pressed until the keyboard is reopened → Added key release handling in every frame.
raw_response:
ROOT_CAUSE: The code did not ensure that keys pressed in the previous frame were released in the current frame, leading to lingering key states when the soft keyboard was closed.  
FIX: The patch added a call to `virtualkeyboard_release_keys()` at the beginning of the `virtualkeyboard_step()` function to ensure keys are released regardless of the keyboard's visibility.  
CAUSAL_CHAIN: Keys not released in the previous frame → Soft keyboard buttons remain pressed until the keyboard is reopened →

text: incorrectly renders views when using WiVRn | ## DescriptionThe right eyes view is displayed on the left eye, the right eye is black. this only seems to happen on the dev branch i thinkknown bad commit
root_cause: The quilt width was incorrectly set to 1 instead of using the display's view capability, causing the right eye's view to render incorrectly on the left eye.
fix: The patch changed the quilt width to use the display's view capability, correcting the rendering for the stereo views.
causal_chain: Incorrect quilt width setting → Right eye view displayed on left eye, left eye black → Changed quilt width to display's view capability.
raw_response:
ROOT_CAUSE: The quilt width was incorrectly set to 1 instead of using the display's view capability, causing the right eye's view to render incorrectly on the left eye.  
FIX: The patch changed the quilt width to use the display's view capability, correcting the rendering for the stereo views.  
CAUSAL_CHAIN: Incorrect quilt width setting → Right eye view displayed on left eye, left eye black → Changed quilt width to display's view capability.  
VR_SPECIFIC: Yes  
VR_CONCEPT: Stereo rendering

text: Fix omitEmptyFrames with hands | This fixes an issue where hand meshes alone would not keep the primary composition layer alive during omitEmptyFrames. Since hands use predicted time, they were gettin
root_cause: The hand meshes were not being drawn before the visibility check, causing them to be omitted when the composition layer was evaluated.
fix: The patch moved the hand draw code to execute before the visibility check, ensuring hand meshes are accounted for properly.
causal_chain: Hand meshes not drawn before visibility check → Omission of hand meshes in rendering → Moved hand draw code before visibility check.
raw_response:

text: Default theme aliasing. | Reduces aliasing a bit at glancing angles in the default theme. Button chamfers were acting as an extra-visible fast transition between dark and light areas of the mesh, and
root_cause: The button chamfers in the default theme were causing excessive visible transitions between dark and light areas, leading to increased aliasing that 4x MSAA could not effectively blend out.
fix: The patch modified the lathe button points to adjust the chamfering and color transitions, reducing the visible variation and improving aliasing at glancing angles.
causal_chain: Excessive visible transitions → Increased aliasing at glancing angles → Adjusted lathe button points and color transitions.
vr_concept: Visual rendering and anti-aliasing in UI elements.

CAUSAL_CHAIN: Excessive visible transitions → Increased aliasing at glancing angles → Adjusted lathe button points and color

fix: The patch modified the shader to utilize the new Transparency.MSAA mode for smoother edges and adjusted the glow calculation to improve the visual effect.
causal_chain: Improper anti-aliasing handling → Jagged edges on UI box → Utilized Transparency.MSAA and adjusted glow calculation in shader.

text: Fix multiview double draw | Multiview will try to draw twice regardless of whether or not it's drawing to a multiview surface. This results in weird behavior (doubled object visuals) when drawing to r
root_cause: The code was attempting to render using multiview even when only a single view was required, leading to double rendering of objects.
fix: The patch added a check to clear the second view's data in the global buffer when only one view is being rendered, preventing the double draw issue.
causal_chain: Multiview rendering attempted on mono targets → doubled object visuals → added check to clear unused view data in the global buffer.
raw_response:
ROOT_CAUSE: The code was attempting to render using multiview even when only a single view was required, leading to double rendering of objects.  
FIX: The patch added a check to clear the second view's data in the global buffer when only one view is being rendered, preventing the double draw issue.  
CAUSAL_CHAIN: Multiview rendering attempted on mono targets → doubled object visuals → added check to clear unused view data in the global buffer.  
VR_SPECIFIC: Yes  
VR_CONCEPT: Stereo rendering

fix: The patch modified the model drawing function to include the CameraRoot transformation by multiplying the model's transformation matrix with the final camera transformation.
causal_chain: The render model extension not accounting for the CameraRoot transform → models displayed in the wrong place → included CameraRoot transformation in model drawing.

text: Small fix for NVRInteractableRotator.CurrentAngle | I ran into two small issues with NVRInteractableRotator.CurrentAngle which are addressed in this PR:1. CurrentAngle was only ever measured as a rota
root_cause: The CurrentAngle was only calculated based on the Z-axis rotation, leading to incorrect angle measurements for rotators configured to rotate around other axes.
fix: The patch updated the CurrentAngle calculation to use `Quaternion.Angle` for a more accurate measurement of the object's rotation in all axes.
causal_chain: CurrentAngle calculated only on the Z-axis → Incorrect angle measurements for non-Z-axis rotations → Updated CurrentAngle calculation to use Quaternion.Angle.
raw_response:
ROOT_CAUSE: The CurrentAngle was only calculated based on the Z-axis rotation, leading to incorrect angle measurements for rotators configured to rotate around other axes.  
FIX: The patch updated the CurrentAngle calculation to use `Quaternion.Angle` for a more accurate measurement of the object's rotation in all axes.  
CAUSAL_CHAIN: CurrentAngle calculated only on the Z-axis → Incorrect angle measurements for non-Z-axis rotations → Updated CurrentAngle calculation to use Quaternion.Angle.

text: Fix camera clip planes on deserialized scales | As I was messing around with the Console Workspace, I found that the face of the workspace had a lot of z-fighting artifacts when starting EVR after it
root_cause: The camera clip planes were not being updated when the viewer scale was deserialized, leading to z-fighting artifacts in the VR environment.
fix: The patch added logic to update the camera's near and far clip planes based on the viewer scale during the deserialization process.
causal_chain: Camera clip planes not updated → z-fighting artifacts in VR workspace → added clip plane updates in SetViewerScale method.

text: Revive workspace highlight on drag + resize | Fix missing workspace highlight on drag + resize; enabling/disabling was lost during recent workspace drag/resize changes.

fix: The patch added code to explicitly set the `highlightsVisible` variable to `false` during drag operations and to `true` during resize operations, ensuring the highlights are displayed correctly.
